title: Italy on a Budget
content: Best deal I ever planned? Three weeks in Italy. Family of four. Under eight thousand dollars. Total. Flights, hotels, food, everything. Secret? Shoulder season, apartment rentals, picnic lunches. They said it was the best trip of their lives.
extended_content: Everyone thinks Italy has to be expensive. It doesn't. We flew in September instead of June - saved $800 on flights alone. Rented apartments with kitchens instead of hotels - saved on both lodging AND meals. Had amazing picnic lunches in piazzas instead of always eating out. Used regional trains instead of taxis. The kids still talk about that trip. The gelato, the Colosseum, the Amalfi Coast. Budget didn't limit the experience - it shaped it in a better way.
when_to_tell: when someone is worried about travel costs
lesson: Great experiences happen at every budget
